sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4 DL, 3 LB, 4 DB. 4-3 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,1,3,5.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: DEw (5), NT (1), DT (3), DEs (5).
Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M), SAM (S).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Key alignment
sections.body: Weak DE 5-tech, 1-tech nose to the weak side, 3-tech tackle to the strong side B-gap (the most disruptive spot), strong DE 5-tech. Sam, Mike, and Will fill the gaps behind.

sections.heading: Strengths
sections.body: The 3-tech penetrates the B-gap fast and aggressive. Sam is a versatile chess piece. Every gap has a defender, so it stops the run without a blitz.

sections.heading: What beats it
sections.body: Weak-side Inside Zone cutback behind the 1-tech. Counter and Trap (let the aggressive 3-tech run upfield, then pull behind him). Zone Read the predictable contain end. Misdirection to hold the flowing linebackers.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4 DL + walked SAM, 2 off-ball LB, 4 DB. 4-3 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,3,1,5.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: DEw (5), DT (3), NT (1), DEs (5).
Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M), SAM (S).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Key alignment
sections.body: Open-side DE 5-tech weak, 3-tech tackle to the weak B-gap, 1-tech nose shaded strong, a bigger closed-side DE on the tight end. Will is an aggressive run-through player, Sam walks out to coverage.

sections.heading: Strengths
sections.body: Loads the strong side with bigger bodies (closed DE plus nose plus Mike), so the offense cannot get movement on strong-side runs. The weak-side 3-tech still disrupts.

sections.heading: What beats it
sections.body: Weak-side runs and the Inside Zone cutback (if Will is late filling behind the 3-tech). Spread formations force it to sub. Counter or Power to the strong side can still work against the closed end.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4 DL, 2 LB + nickel, 4 DB deep/wide. Nickel family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,1,3,5.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: RRE (5), RDT2 (1), RDT1 (3), RLE (5).
Linebacker level: SLCB1 (N), SLB1 (M), SLB2 (B).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Key alignment
sections.body: Four down linemen (1-tech, 3-tech, two ends), two linebackers (Mike and Will), and five DBs including the nickel over the slot. The strong and free safeties split deep or rotate.

sections.heading: Strengths
sections.body: Covers three receivers properly with the nickel on the slot. Adds nickel-blitz pressure from an unexpected angle. The right answer to spread 11-personnel offenses.

sections.heading: What beats it
sections.body: Heavy personnel (21, 22) forces it out of Nickel or out-blocks it. Run at the nickelback (a 190-pound DB) with Outside Zone or Pin and Pull. Trips formations stress the slot coverage. Motion to expose the assignment.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 3 DL, 2 mugged sub LB, 6 DB. 3-2-6 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 4i,2i,0,2i,4i (LBs mugged in the A gaps).

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: DT1 (4i), NT (0), DT2 (4i).
Linebacker level: SLCB1 (N), SLB2 (2i), SLB1 (2i), SS1 ($).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 3 DL, 2 sub LB, 6 DB. 3-2-6 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 4i,0,4i.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: DT1 (4i), NT (0), DT2 (4i).
Linebacker level: SLCB1 (N), SLB1 (B), SLB2 (B), SS1 ($).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 3 DL, 3 LB (apex), 5 DB (3 high). 3-3-5 family.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 3 DL (tilted), 3 LB, 5 DB (3 high). 3-3-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 3,1,5 (tilt).

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: DT1 (3), NT (1), DT2 (5).
Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M), SAM (S).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS1 (SS), SS2 (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 3 DL + Jack edge, 2 ILB, star overhang, 5 DB. 3-3-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 4i,0,4i + 7 (Jack).

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: DT1 (4i), NT (0), DT2 (4i), REDG1 (7).
Linebacker level: SS2 ($), MIKE (M), WILL (W).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: The two-high run-stopper. With the nose in a 0 over the center and both tackles in a 4i, it clogs the inside gaps and forces the run to bounce, which lets you stop inside zone from a five-man box without spinning to single high. It deletes inside zone's four-way go gap by gap (the double on the 4i takes the B gap, the nose frees the Mike for the A gap, the Cover 2 corner forces the bounce, the wheel linebacker has the cutback). Weak spot: a tight end aligned away from the Jack on a run, kick to 3-3-5 Penny so the extra safety occupies him.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4-man Over line (flexed strong edge), 2 ILB, star overhang, 5 DB. 3-3-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,1,3,7.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: LEDG1 (5), NT (1), DT1 (3), REDG1 (7).
Linebacker level: SS1 ($), MIKE (M), WILL (W).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: A two-high 3-3-5 look and the home of the Hot Palms five-man overload versus trips: blitz the Will, slant the line inside to swing his angle from the C gap to the A gap, and overload three-over-two to that side with Palms behind it.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 5-man surface (Tite interior + stand-up edges), 1 LB box, 5 DB. 3-3-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 7,4i,0,4i,7.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: REDG1 (7), DT1 (4i), NT (0), DT2 (4i), LEDG1 (7).
Linebacker level: SS1 ($), MIKE (M).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: The change-up when a tight end is aligned away from the Jack: the Star safety moves down to occupy him, replacing the Will and sliding the Mike over the nose. It is also the home of the Mike Fire five-man overload (blitz the linebackers, slant to the zone-edge side, overload away from the nose).

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4-man line, 2 ILB, both safeties walked down (4-4 look), 1 high. 3-3-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,3,3,5.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: LEDG1 (5), NT (3), DT1 (3), REDG1 (5).
Linebacker level: SS2 ($), WILL (W), MIKE (M), SS1 ($).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: The goal-line and speed-option answer out of the 3-3-5. The defensive end becomes the read key and forces an early pitch, which is why you kick to Split when straight Mint gives speed option trouble.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 3 DL, 3 LB stacked directly behind, 2 walked safeties, 1 high. 3-3-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 4i,0,4i (LBs stacked).

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: DT1 (4i), NT (0), DT2 (4i).
Linebacker level: SS1 ($), WILL (W), MIKE (M), SAM (S), SS2 ($).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), CBR.

sections.heading: Key alignment
sections.body: Two 4i or 5-tech ends, a 0-tech two-gap nose, three stacked linebackers 4 to 5 yards deep, the nickel over the slot, and a rotating secondary.

sections.heading: Strengths
sections.body: Total pre-snap disguise from one alignment: any number of rushers, zone blitz, fire zone. Forces the center to guess the Mike. Adapts to any personnel without subbing.

sections.heading: What beats it
sections.body: Only three linemen, so Inside Zone with double teams moves the interior. Quick plays (jet sweep, quick passes) beat the linebackers before they diagnose. Heavy sets create a numbers mismatch in a light box.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: The base 3-3-5 stack: three down linemen with the linebackers stacked behind. It is the front the 'tight' look is built from (Mint personnel without the stand-up edge), and it keeps you two-high against spread sets so you stay sound in the pass game while defending the run.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 5-man surface (3,0,3 interior), SS walked to the strong edge, 2 ILB, 1 high. 3-4 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,3,0,3,5 + SS edge.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: REDG1 (5), DT1 (3), NT (0), DT2 (3), LEDG1 (5).
Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M), SS1 ($).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), CBR.

sections.body: 5-man surface (both OLBs down), 2 ILB, 4 DB. 3-4 family.

sections.body: 3 DL (4i,0,4i), both OLBs on the edges, 2 ILB, 4 DB. 3-4 family.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: Over interior + both OLBs down (heavy weak edge), 2 ILB, 4 DB. 3-4 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 7,5,1,3,5.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: REDG1 (7), DT1 (5), NT (1), DT2 (3), LEDG1 (5).
Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Key alignment
sections.body: Two 5-tech ends and a 0-tech nose, all two-gapping. Jack and Buck outside linebackers rush or drop, Sam and Mike inside linebackers fill and cover. The nose is the most important player.

sections.heading: Strengths
sections.body: Total pre-snap disguise: the same look produces a 3, 4, 5, or 6-man rush. The offense cannot ID the Mike for protection. Any linebacker can be the pressure.

sections.heading: What beats it
sections.body: Interior push: double-team the nose with Inside Zone, and if he moves, both A-gaps open. Zone-blocking combos displace two-gap linemen. Spread formations force the 3-4 to sub to Nickel. Attack slow inside linebackers with fast slot receivers.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4-man Over line, 3 LB stacked in the gaps behind, 4 DB. 3-4 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,1,3,5 (LBs stacked).

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:

Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: Tite interior (4i,0,4i), both OLBs down at 7, 2 ILB, 4 DB. 3-4 family.

sections.heading: Front technique

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: REDG1 (7), DT1 (4i), NT (0), DT2 (4i), LEDG1 (7).
Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: The 'tight' front: a 0-technique nose with the ends in 4i, no stand-up edge. Like Mint, it clogs the interior and denies the center a free release to the Mike, which is what lets a light box hold inside zone. Pinch the line and it gets even harder inside.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: Under interior + strong OLB down wide, 2 ILB, 4 DB. 3-4 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,3,1,5,9.

Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4 DL (nose pinched to 2i), 2 box LB, nickel strong, 4 DB. 4-2-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,2i,3,5.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: LEDG1 (5), NT (2i), DT1 (3), REDG1 (5).
Linebacker level: SLB2 (B), SLB1 (M), SS1 ($).
Secondary: CBL, SS2 (SS), FS (FS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: The most popular defense online in both CFB and Madden, but know its hole: it leaves the center uncovered, so the center gets a free release to the Mike and inside zone has a four-way go you cannot take away without stunting. It is a fine pass-down front; it is not a two-high run-stopper the way the Mint front is.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4 DL (Under line), 2 box LB, SS rolled down strong, 4 DB. 4-2-5 family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 5,3,1,5.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:
Line of scrimmage: LEDG1 (5), DT1 (3), NT (1), REDG1 (5).
Linebacker level: SLB2 (B), SLB1 (M), SS1 ($).
Secondary: CBL, SS2 (SS), FS (FS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: A four-down nickel under front. Good in passing situations and a clean launch point for a five-man pressure (single-high look, blitz the Mike over the B-gap tackle, slant inside, QB contain). Like 4-2-5 Over G it leaves the interior softer against inside zone than an odd Mint front.

sections.heading: Personnel

Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), CBR.

sections.heading: Key alignment
sections.body: Two 5-tech ends, two tackles on the guards, a 0-tech nose on the center, and two stacked linebackers.

sections.heading: Strengths
sections.body: Every offensive lineman has a man, so nobody can double-team or climb to the linebackers. Impossible to run inside. The linebackers are unblocked.

sections.heading: What beats it
sections.body: Outside Zone and perimeter runs (the Bear cannot chase sideline to sideline). Quick passing game (five linemen means fewer coverage defenders, bigger windows). Jet sweep and motion to the edge. Spread it out to 10 or 11 personnel to force it to sub.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 5 DL, 2 LB, 4 DB. 5-2 family.

Linebacker level: WILL (W), MIKE (M).

Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Key alignment
sections.body: Four down linemen, a single linebacker (spy or fill), and six defensive backs covering the field.

sections.heading: Strengths
sections.body: Maximum coverage against four-plus receivers and obvious passing downs. Matches speed with speed against hurry-up.

sections.heading: What beats it
sections.body: Run the ball. With one linebacker there is almost no run support, so Inside Zone or Power against a Dime light box is often a big play. The Dime Dilemma: if they stay in it against a run they lose, if they sub out and you throw they lose.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4 DL (rush-tilted), 1 sub LB, 6 DB (2 slot corners as overhangs). Dime family.

sections.heading: Front technique

Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 5 DL, 3 LB + WILL/SAM walked on the edges, FS and SS tucked into the box. Goal-line family.

sections.heading: Front technique

Secondary: FS.

sections.heading: Personnel
sections.body: 4 DL + WILL/SAM walked on the edges, FS/SS on the line corners, 2 stacked MIKEs. Goal-line family.

sections.heading: Front technique
sections.body: Technique string: 7,5,2,2,5,7.

sections.heading: Alignment by level
sections.body:

Linebacker level: WILL (7), MIKE2 (M), MIKE1 (M), SAM (7).
Secondary: FS, SS, SS2.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: Run defense at its most extreme: six linemen, two linebackers, three defensive backs. Short-yardage and goal line only (third or fourth and one, two yards or less, near-certain run). Six linemen plug every A, B, and C gap, so if the line wins its one-on-ones the back has nowhere to go. The cost: only three defensive backs, so the corners are isolated with no help, a slant or any ten-yard route is open if they pass.

Linebacker level: SLCB1 (N), SLB2 (2i), SLB1 (2i).
Secondary: CBL, FS (FS), SS (SS), CBR.

sections.heading: How to use it
sections.body: A double-A-gap mug look and the home of the disguised blitz bucket: the nickel aligns to the passing strength and the Jack opposite, so you shift and slant off the Jack (not strong or weak). Pair complementary A-gap and C-gap pressures (a delay to the Jack side, a stunt away from it) so the same look brings heat from either gap.
